source: DPWH Resilient Design Manual, PAGASA, CCC, Industry Practice 2024-2026

rules.description: Amphibious or flood-adaptive foundations for coastal and riparian zones
rules.notes: Houses designed to float or rise with floodwaters, then settle back. Buoyant foundation systems piloted in Tacloban and Leyte post-Yolanda. Combining bahay kubo stilt wisdom with modern engineering.

rules.description: Breakaway ground floor walls for storm surge zones
rules.notes: Non-structural ground floor walls designed to break away under surge force, preserving the structure above. Living spaces on second floor. Adopted from US FEMA V-zone guidelines, adapted for PH.

rules.description: Multi-hazard safe rooms integrated into residential design
rules.notes: Reinforced interior room (typically bathroom or storage) rated for typhoon winds 250+ kph. Doubles as secure storage. Trending in Visayas and eastern Luzon new builds.

rules.description: Modular and demountable construction for rapid post-disaster rebuild
rules.notes: Pre-engineered steel frame + bolt-on panel systems that can be disassembled, relocated, or rapidly replaced. Schools, clinics, and temporary housing. Growing post-disaster housing market.

rules.description: Solar + battery microgrids for residential off-grid or grid-resilient design
rules.notes: 5kWp solar + 10kWh battery provides 24-48hr autonomy during grid outages (common after typhoons). Island and rural resort projects increasingly designed off-grid from the start. LFP batteries dropping below PHP 15,000/kWh.

rules.description: Net-zero energy homes — emerging in PH high-end residential
rules.notes: Solar PV + passive cooling + high-performance envelope achieving net-zero annual energy. Still rare but growing — PHILGBC showcased 3 certified net-zero homes in 2025. Tropical advantage: cooling load solved passively.

rules.description: Passive survivability — habitable without mechanical systems for 72+ hours
rules.notes: Building remains thermally habitable (below 31°C) for 72 hours without power. Achieved through cross ventilation, thermal mass management, shading, and potable water storage. Post-typhoon design criterion.

rules.description: Rainwater-as-primary-source design for island and rural buildings
rules.notes: Full rainwater harvesting system with filtration, UV treatment, and 30-day storage. Standard for Palawan, Siargao, and Guimaras resort builds where municipal water is unreliable.

rules.description: Atmospheric water generators as supplemental source in humid climates
rules.notes: PH humidity (80-90% RH) is ideal for AWG. Units produce 20-50L/day from air. Emerging trend in high-end residential and office buildings as backup potable source.

rules.description: Base isolation and seismic dampers in mid-rise residential
rules.notes: Previously only high-rise/institutional. Now trickling down to 5-10 storey condos in Metro Manila and Cebu. Lead-rubber bearings and friction pendulum systems. West Valley Fault awareness driving demand.

rules.description: Performance-based seismic design replacing prescriptive code compliance
rules.notes: NSCP 2015 allows performance-based design for complex structures. Non-linear analysis with target performance levels (Immediate Occupancy, Life Safety, Collapse Prevention). Growing adoption in BGC and Makati towers.
